title: M&E Engineer
description:
World Health SafetyTec (WHST) is a Singapore-based company providing EHS Consultation, Biological Indicator (BI) sterility testing, bizSAFE 3 certification support, and outsourcing of Safety & Medical Personnel. 

We partner with clients and businesses to strengthen workplace safety, regulatory compliance, and operational excellence.

For this position, selected candidate will be stationed onsite (Tuas) with a client partner as part of our outsourced services. This key role to provide technical support and perform preventive and corrective maintenance for all mechanical, electrical and building systems to ensure the facility operates safely, efficiently, and in compliance with applicable standards.

Key Responsibilities:

1. Maintenance & Repairs

i. Perform routine preventive maintenance on HVAC, plumbing, lighting, fire protection and electrical systems.

ii. Troubleshoot and repair electrical circuits, panels, motors, pumps and other building equipment.

iii. iv. Carry out minor plumbing, air-conditioning and carpentry repairs.

Assist with lift, generator and UPS system inspections and servicing.

2. Monitoring & Operations

i. Monitor Building Management System (BMS) systems for alerts and anomalies.

ii. iii. Respond promptly to alarms, power failures or mechanical system issues. Operate equipment safely and ensure all readings and conditions are recorded accurately.

3. Compliance & Documentation

i. Ensure compliance with safety regulations and internal SOPs.

ii. Maintain logs, checklists and maintenance records.

iii. Support audits and inspections by authorities or internal stakeholders.

4. Coordination

i. Coordinate with vendors or contractors for specialized repair or contract services work.

ii. Support facilities team during shutdowns, renovations and new installations.

iii. Liaise with tenants or building occupants to minimize disruption during maintenance work.

5. Emergency Support

i. Provide emergency response for M&E failures or breakdowns.

ii. Participate in 24/7 duty roster or standby support if required.

Qualification & Experience Requirements:

1 Education & Experience:

ITE, Higher ITE, or Diploma in Mechanical / Electrical / Building Services Engineering or related field.

2 Key Skills & Attributes:

i. At least 2 years of relevant experience in building maintenance, facilities management, or a similar role.

ii. Hands-on experience with HVAC, electrical systems, or plumbing is highly preferred

3 Skills & Competencies

i. Basic knowledge of M&E drawings, schematics, and technical manuals.

ii. Familiarity with CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System) is an advantage.

iii. iv. Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail. Physically fit and able to work at heights or in confined spaces, when required.

4 Other Requirements

i. Willingness to work shifts, weekends or after-hours as needed.

ii. Valid electrical license (e.g Licensed Electrical Worker - LEW) is a plus.

Please include your current and expected salary in your resume. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Thank you for your understanding.
